segments.speaker: JEANNE
segments.en: How painful that must have been!

segments.speaker: THOMAS
segments.en: Yeah, well, I think we can put together some useful ideas ...
segments.zh: 嗯，我觉得我们可以整理出一些有用的点子……

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Hello everyone.
segments.zh: 大家好。今天我们要看另一种天然食品——枫糖浆。这到底是什么呢？枫糖浆看起来像透明的蜂蜜，但不是蜜蜂制造的；它来自枫树内部的植物液体（或树液），因此枫糖浆是一种非常天然的产品。枫糖浆是一种浓稠、金黄、味甜的液体，可以装在瓶子或罐子里购买，浇在食物上，比如华夫饼和冰淇淋，或者用于烘焙蛋糕和糕点。它不含防腐剂或添加成分，是精制糖的健康替代品。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Today we're going to look at another natural food product and that's maple syrup.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: What is this exactly?

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Well, maple syrup looks rather like clear honey, but it's not made by bees; it's produced from the plant fluid – or sap – inside the maple tree and that makes maple syrup a very natural product.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Maple syrup is a thick, golden, sweet-tasting liquid that can be bought in bottles or jars and poured onto food such as waffles and ice cream or used in the baking of cakes and pastries.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It contains no preservatives or added ingredients, and it provides a healthy alternative to refined sugar.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Let's just talk a bit about the maple tree itself, which is where maple syrup comes from.
segments.zh: 我们先来谈谈枫树本身，枫糖浆就是从它来的。枫树有很多品种，在土壤水分充足的地区，它们不需要肥料就能生长。但是，只有满足另一个重要条件时它们才能生长，那就是白天要有充足或部分阳光照射，夜晚要非常凉爽——我一会儿再详细讲。世界上只有某些地区具备所有这些条件：一个是加拿大，我指的是加拿大的所有地区，另一个是北美东北部的各州。在这些地区，气候非常适合枫树生长。事实上，加拿大生产了世界上超过三分之二的枫糖浆，这就是为什么五角枫叶是加拿大的象征，并且自1964年以来一直出现在国旗上。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So, there are many species of maple tree, and they'll grow without fertilizer in areas where there's plenty of moisture in the soil.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: However, they'll only do this if another important criterion is fulfilled, which is that they must have full or partial sun exposure during the day and very cool nights – and I'll talk more about that in a minute.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: There are only certain parts of the world that provide all these conditions: one is Canada, and by that, I mean all parts of Canada, and the other is the north-eastern states of North America.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In these areas, the climate suits the trees perfectly.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In fact, Canada produces over two-thirds of the world's maple syrup, which is why the five-pointed maple leaf is a Canadian symbol and has featured on the flag since 1964.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So how did maple syrup production begin?
segments.zh: 那么枫糖浆的生产是如何开始的呢？早在欧洲人定居在这些地区之前，原住民社区就已经开始生产枫糖了。他们在枫树的树干上钻孔，用树皮制成的容器收集流出的液体树液。由于他们无法长时间保存液体——那时他们没有储存设施——他们通过将白天被太阳晒得滚烫的岩石放入树液中来煮沸树液。他们这样做直到树液变成糖，然后就可以用它来给食物和饮料增甜。从那时起，这个过程有所改进，但总体上变化很小。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Well, long before Europeans settled in these parts of the world, the indigenous communities had started producing maple sugar.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: They bored holes in the trunks of maple trees and used containers made of tree bark to collect the liquid sap as it poured out.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: As they were unable to keep the liquid for any length of time – they didn't have storage facilities in those days – they boiled the liquid by placing pieces of rock that had become scorching hot from the sun into the sap.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: They did this until it turned into sugar, and they were then able to use this to sweeten their food and drinks.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Since that time, improvements have been made to the process, but it has changed very little overall.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So let's look at the production of maple syrup today.
segments.zh: 那么让我们看看今天的枫糖浆生产。显然，枫树林是许多加拿大和北美社区的宝贵资源。这些树必须得到很好的照料，并且直到树干直径达到大约25厘米时才能用来制作糖浆。这可能需要长达40年的时间。正如我已经提到的，枫树需要合适的条件才能生长并产生树液。这是为什么呢？嗯，情况是这样的：在寒冷的夜晚，树从土壤中吸收水分，水分通过树的维管系统上升。但在较暖的白天，温度的变化导致水分被推回树的底部。这种持续的上下运动导致了枫糖浆生产所需的树液的形成。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Clearly, the maple forests are a valuable resource in many Canadian and North American communities.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The trees have to be well looked after and they cannot be used to make syrup until the trunks reach a diameter of around 25 centimetres.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This can take anything up to 40 years.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: As I've already mentioned, maple trees need the right conditions to grow and also to produce sap.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Why is this?

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Well, what happens is that during a cold night, the tree absorbs water from the soil, and that rises through the tree's vascular system.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: But then in the warmer daytime, the change in temperature causes the water to be pushed back down to the bottom of the tree.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This continual movement – up and down – leads to the formation of the sap needed for maple syrup production.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: When the tree is ready, it can be tapped and this involves drilling a small hole into the trunk and inserting a tube into it that ends in a bucket.
segments.zh: 当树准备好时，就可以进行 tapping，这包括在树干上钻一个小孔，插入一根管子，管子末端连接一个桶。这些树通常可以承受多次 tapping，尽管工人们会小心不损害树的健康生长。从树中流出的树液由98%的水和2%的糖及其他营养物质组成。必须煮沸使大部分水蒸发，这个过程必须立即进行，使用所谓的蒸发器。这些基本上是非常大的平底锅——将树液倒入其中，生火，然后平底锅被

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The trees can often take several taps, though the workers take care not to cause any damage to the healthy growth of the tree itself.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The sap that comes out of the trees consists of 98 percent water and 2 percent sugar and other nutrients.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It has to be boiled so that much of that water evaporates, and this process has to take place immediately, using what are called evaporators.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: These are basically extremely large pans – the sap is poured into these, a fire is built and the pans are then

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: heated until the sap boils.
segments.zh: 加热直到树液沸腾。在这个过程中，水蒸发，糖浆开始形成。蒸发过程产生大量蒸汽，树液变得更浓稠、密度更大，在恰到好处的时刻，当树液足够浓稠可以称为枫糖浆时，工人将其从火上移开。这个过程之后，必须过滤掉所谓的“糖砂”，因为它在煮沸过程中积聚，使糖浆看起来浑浊，口感略带沙粒感。一旦完成，糖浆就可以包装，用于各种产品。生产一升枫糖浆需要40升树液，所以你可以想象需要多少！

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: As it does this, the water evaporates, and the syrup begins to form.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The evaporation process creates large quantities of steam, and the sap becomes thicker and denser, and, at just the right moment, when the sap is thick enough to be called maple syrup, the worker removes it from the heat.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: After this process, something called 'sugar sand' has to be filtered out as this builds up during the boiling and gives the syrup a cloudy appearance and a slightly gritty taste.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Once this has been done, the syrup is ready to be packaged so that it can be used for a whole variety of products.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It takes 40 litres of sap to produce one litre of maple syrup so you can get an idea of how much is needed!

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So that's the basic process.
segments.zh: 所以这就是基本流程。在像魁北克这样的地方，那里……

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In places like Quebec where ...

pairs.note: 原文直接用了golden描述颜色，与题干colour对应。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问颜色描述，原文中'golden'直接对应颜色，因此定位到该词。

pairs.note: 题干中的'very ... compared to'对应原文的'alternative to'，表示替代关系。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句最后一句提到'healthy alternative to refined sugar'，与题干'very ... compared to refined sugar'对应，其中'healthy'就是答案。

items.prompt: best growing conditions and ... are in Canada and North America

pairs.note: 题干说'最佳生长条件'，原文用'all these conditions'指代前文提到的光照、凉爽夜晚等条件，是概括性替换。
题干用'are in'表示存在，原文用'provide'表示提供，意思相同。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句先描述枫树需要的条件，然后说只有某些地方提供这些条件，接着列举加拿大和北美东北部，最后总结'在这些地区，气候非常适合枫树'，因此答案'climate'对应题干中的'best growing conditions'。

items.prompt: used hot ... to heat the sap

pairs.note: scorching hot 是 hot 的强调说法，表示非常热。

pairs.p: boiled the liquid by placing pieces of rock that had become scorching hot from the sun into the sap
pairs.note: 原文用 boiling 和 placing hot rocks into the sap 描述了加热 sap 的过程。
rock 直接对应，是加热用的石头。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问用什么加热 sap，原文说用 scorching hot 的 rock 放入 sap 中煮沸，所以答案 rock。

items.prompt: Tree trunks may not have the correct ... until they have been growing for 40 years.

pairs.note: 题干中的correct ...对应原文中的diameter，直接匹配。
题干说生长40年，原文说达到直径需要最多40年，意思相同。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到树干直径达到25厘米才能用于制糖，这个过程最多需要40年，与题干中树干需要生长40年才能达到正确直径一致。

items.prompt: a ... carries the sap into a bucket

pairs.q: carries the sap into a bucket
pairs.p: inserting a tube into it that ends in a bucket
pairs.note: 题干说'把树液运进桶里'，原文说'插入一根管子，末端在桶里'，意思相同。

paraphrase.explain: 定位到原文中描述 tapping 过程的句子，其中 tube 是插入树干并通向桶的装置，对应题干中的 a ... carries the sap into a bucket。

pairs.note: 原文中'fire is built'对应题干'heated by means of a fire'，'fire'是加热源。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问加热方式，定位句提到'fire is built'，直接对应'fire'。

items.prompt: A lot of ... is produced during the evaporation process.

pairs.note: large quantities of 替换 a lot of，表示大量
creates 替换 produced，表示产生
原文直接出现 The evaporation process，与题干对应

paraphrase.explain: 定位句明确提到 'The evaporation process creates large quantities of steam'，其中 'large quantities of' 对应 'a lot of'，'creates' 对应 'produced'，'steam' 即为答案。

items.prompt: 'Sugar sand' is removed because it makes the syrup look ... and affects the taste.

pairs.p: gives the syrup a cloudy appearance
pairs.note: 原文'gives...a cloudy appearance'对应题干'makes...look cloudy'，'cloudy'是关键词。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'sugar sand'被过滤掉，因为它导致糖浆外观浑浊，题干问的是外观如何，所以答案就是'cloudy'。

items.prompt: A huge quantity of sap is needed to make a ... of maple syrup.

pairs.note: 题干说大量 sap，原文用具体数字 40 litres 说明，是数量上的同义替换。
题干缺的词就是 litre，原文直接出现 one litre，是原词复现。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句最后一句明确说需要 40 litres of sap 才能 produce one litre of maple syrup，与题干“大量 sap 才能做一... maple syrup”对应，答案就是 litre。
